text.title: agentic-rag-query-decomposition Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for agentic-rag-query-decomposition. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Sub-query decomposition MUST produce 2–4 sub-queries. If the LLM returns fewer than 2, treat the original query as atomic and fall back to single-pass RAG. If it returns more than 4, keep only the 4 highest-confidence sub-queries.
rule.rationale: Fewer than 2 sub-queries means the original question was already atomic; more than 4 leads to redundant overlapping retrievals that inflate token usage and context length without improving coverage.

rule.statement: Run independent sub-queries in parallel (e.g., via asyncio.gather or a thread pool). Cache sub-query results within a single agentic run — the same sub-query may recur across planning iterations.
rule.rationale: Sub-queries are independent by construction; serial execution adds one round-trip latency per sub-query unnecessarily. Caching prevents duplicate retrieval calls when the same sub-query is generated in a later refinement cycle.

rule.statement: Track sub-query coverage: each sub-query MUST retrieve at least 1 high-confidence chunk (score above retrieval threshold). If a sub-query returns zero high-confidence chunks, flag it for query expansion rather than silently dropping it.
rule.rationale: Silent coverage gaps produce answers that look complete but are missing entire information domains. Explicit flagging surfaces corpus holes for human review or corpus update.
